result.summary: Salesforce exposes a tenant-scoped GraphQL API, Salesforce-hosted MCP servers, and the `sf` CLI; all documented developer surfaces found require Salesforce OAuth-based authentication.
result.description: Salesforce is a cloud CRM and application platform for managing customer data, workflows, and business applications. Organizations use it to build and run custom apps, automate processes, and integrate with Salesforce data and services.

sf_oauth_app.label: Salesforce external client app / connected app OAuth credentials
sf_oauth_app.setup: Create an External Client App in Salesforce Setup via [Exercise 1: Configure Salesforce MCP Servers](https://developer.salesforce.com/workshops/headless-360-workshop/explore-salesforce-hosted-mcps/1-enable-and-connect) or use a custom connected app as described in [org login web](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/salesforce-cli-reference/guide/cli_reference_org_login_web.html). Enable OAuth and grant the scopes your integration needs. For hosted MCP access, the docs show the `mcp_api` scope; for long-lived access they also show `refresh_token` / `offline_access`. Use your org My Domain URL like `https://<MyDomainName>.my.salesforce.com` as the Salesforce instance for authorization flows.

sf_access_token.label: Salesforce OAuth access token
sf_access_token.setup: Get an API access token using Salesforce OAuth 2.0. The easiest path in the docs is the quick start in [Connect Altair GraphQL Client to Salesforce](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/graphql/guide/get-started-graphql.html), which says GraphQL uses OAuth 2.0 and recommends authenticating via Salesforce CLI for quick starts; for production apps, create your own [external client app](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.external_client_apps.htm) and use an OAuth flow that fits your app. The token is then sent as `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.

sf_cli_login.label: Salesforce CLI authorized org session
sf_cli_login.setup: Install the CLI from [Salesforce CLI](https://developer.salesforce.com/tools/salesforcecli), then run `sf org login web` as documented in [org login web](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/salesforce-cli-reference/guide/cli_reference_org_login_web.html). This opens a browser to a Salesforce login page and authorizes the CLI for that org. You can optionally pass `--instance-url https://<MyDomainName>.my.salesforce.com` for My Domain orgs or `--client-id` to use your own connected app.

requiredHeaders.name: X-Chatter-Entity-Encoding
requiredHeaders.description: Required by the GraphQL quick start example for proper encoding.

variables.name: MyDomainName
variables.resolveFrom: your Salesforce org My Domain subdomain
variables.description: Custom subdomain for the Salesforce org.

variables.name: version
variables.description: REST/GraphQL API version, such as v57.0.

variables.name: org_mcp_endpoint
variables.resolveFrom: the hosted MCP server endpoint activated in your Salesforce org
variables.description: The workshop confirms Salesforce hosts MCP servers in your org, but the exact connect endpoint URL was not exposed in the retrieved docs.
